title: Bridge Essay: Neural Mechanisms and Psychology of Psychedelic Ego Dissolution.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ultimate/Emptiness** (Phase 17) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Ultimate/Emptiness to mechanistic neuroscience through Neural, Mechanisms, and, Psychology, of.

**Neural Mechanisms and Psychology of Psychedelic Ego Dissolution.** — Pharmacol Rev. PMID: 36786290. DOI: 10.1124/pharmrev.121.000508. Neuroimaging studies of psychedelics have advanced our understanding of hierarchical brain organization and the mechanisms underlying their subjective and therapeutic effects. The primary mechanism of action of classic psychedelics is binding to serotonergic 5-HT2A receptors. Agonist activity at these receptors leads to neuromodulatory changes in synaptic efficacy that can have aprofound effect on hierarchical message-passing in the brain. Here, we review the cognitive and neuroimaging evidence
